source_lists.title: Cosmic Horror
source_lists.desc:
"The most merciful thing in the world, I think, is the inability of the human mind to correlate all its contents. We live on a placid island of ignorance in the midst of black seas of infinity, and it was not meant that we should voyage far. The sciences, each straining in its own direction, have hitherto harmed us little; but some day the piecing together of dissociated knowledge will open up such terrifying vistas of reality, and of our frightful position therein, that we shall either go mad from the revelation or flee from the deadly light into the peace and safety of a new dark age."

- H.P. Lovecraft, "The Call of Cthulhu"

source_lists.title: Cosmic Horror
source_lists.desc: List of horrror and science-fiction films with lovecraftian themes consisting of (but not entirely including); eldritch abominations, cosmic dread, cults, fear of the unknown, etc. Some films in this list are also straight up adaptations of H.P. Lovecraft as well.

source_lists.title: Cosmic Horror
source_lists.desc:
the unknown brushing up against the known; exhibits on the insignificance of humanity; eldritch abominations; cosmic dread; obscure cults and rituals; fear of the unknown; straight Lovecraft adaptations; made in the spirit of Lovecraft but not based on his works; human regression and lost races; Necronomicon; Egyptian mythos horror; tentacled, misshapen life-forms; gateways to hellish alternate dimensions 

Companion lists:
+ Catholic Horror - Christian-style hellscapes and Satanic cults
+ Extraterrestrial - meeting of alien and human species
+ Not of This Earth - set on spaceships, space stations, other planets, and alien worlds

source_lists.title: Cosmic Horror
source_lists.desc:
This is *hands down* my favorite subgenre. Cosmic (or Lovecraftian) horror that leaves you unsettled, not knowing what is out there. Cthulhu, Eldritch terrors, body mutations, a morphing disease/infestation, psychological meltdowns, the void.... "Unexplainable dread of outer, unknown forces"

Check out my Cosmic Horror playlist on Spotify: open.spotify.com/playlist/4FtliPjFvY1Db1By81UR1V?si=60f9025274b04d49

source_lists.title: Cosmic Horror
source_lists.desc:
Last Updated:
--Added Alien
--Added Aliens


QUALIFIERS:
---AT LEAST TWO OF:

-Abominations: Tentacles, body horror, strange creatures, etc.
---(Ex: The Thing, From Beyond)
-Existential Dread: Cosmological nihilism, hopelessness, fridge horror.
---(Ex: The Mist, Under the Skin)
-Evil/Dead God(s): Ancient, all-powerful evils. Alien minds that cannot be understood.
---(Ex: The Borderlands, When Evil Lurks)
-Madness: Knowledge that makes you crazy. Visages that cannot be unseen.
---(Ex: The Lighthouse)
-Cyclopean Environment: Non-euclidian geometries. Labyrinths that fold on themselves.
---(Ex: Mad God, Relic, Skinamarink)
-The Indescribable: Things that cannot be understood, much less communicated.
---(Ex: Annihilation)

---BOTH OF:

-I've seen it
-I liked it (some exceptions)

SORTED BY:

-How Cosmic Horror Is it?
--A Tier: Overtly cosmic horror films that strongly lean in…
